perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information disclosed by Discloser shall remain the property of such Discloser, and no license or other rights to such Discloser ’s Confidential Information is granted or implied hereby. Recipient shall reproduce the symbols, legends or other p roprietary notices affixed to Confidential Information, and shall not, nor permit any third party to, remove, add or modify the same .
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information disclosed by Discloser shall become the property of the Recipient upon disclosure, granting the Recipient full license and rights to use and modify such Confidential Information as they see fit. Recipient is not required to reproduce the symbols, legends or other proprietary notices affixed to Confidential Information, and may permit any third party to remove, add or modify the same.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original statement that Confidential Information remains the property of the Discloser. Now it states the opposite: the Recipient owns it upon disclosure. Also, by stating 'granting the Recipient full license and rights to use and modify such Confidential Information as they see fit' contradicts the limitations and protections outlined in Section 2. Nondisclosure and Nonuse Obligations. The original Section 4 clearly establishes that Discloser retains ownership, while the perturbation gives ownership to the recipient which is contradicting that fact.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nondisclosure and Nonuse Obligations . Each of the parties, as Recipient , hereby promise s and agree s to receive and hold Confidential Information in confidence, and to protect and safeguard Confidential Information against unauthorized use or disclosure using at least the same degree of care as Recipient accords to its own confidential information of lik e importance, but in no case less than reasonable care. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, each party, as Recipient , further promise s and agree s: (a) not to, directly or indirectly, in any way, disclose, make accessible, reveal, report, pub lish, disseminate or transfer any Confidential Inf ormation to any third party; (b) not to use any Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever, except in furtherance of the Purpose in acc ordance with this Agreement; and (c) to restrict access to Confi dential Information to those of its officers, directors and employees who have a legitimate need - to-know to carry out the Purpose and who are obligated to protect such Confidential Information pursuant to terms and conditions no less protective of Disclose r than those contained in this Agreement ; and (d) not to reproduce or copy Confidential Information except to the extent necessary to further the Purpose . Confidential Furthermore, the existence of any business negotiati ons, discussions or agreements in progress between the parties shall be kept confidential and shall not be disclosed without written approval of all the parties . For the purposes of this Agreement, Company shall not disclose Confidential Information to any other division, department, group, or unit other than Samsung KNOX Business and Technology Division .
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text and the contradicted text both establish that the Discloser retains ownership and control of the Confidential Information, while the changed text explicitly transfers ownership and rights to the Recipient, contradicting the Nondisclosure and Nonuse Obligations outlined in the contradicted section. This establishes a direct conflict based on Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, according to the definitions.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be effective from the Effective Date until 24 months thereafter. Either party may terminate this Agreement for any or no reason upon 10 days written notice to the other party. However, any termination of this Agreement shall not relieve Recipient of its confidentiality and use obligations with respec t to Confidential Information disclosed prior to the date of such termination.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be effective from the Effective Date and continue indefinitely, with no termination clause. However, any termination of this Agreement shall relieve Recipient of its confidentiality and use obligations with respec t to Confidential Information disclosed prior to the date of such termination.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies a term of 24 months and allows termination with 10 days' notice, while the change makes the agreement indefinite and removes the termination clause and makes the Recipient relieved of its confidentialty. This directly contradicts the originally agreed upon term and contradicts the confidentiality obligations. It creates uncertainty about the duration of the agreement and the conditions under which confidentiality obligations cease. The insertion contradict the original clause stating: 'However, any termination of this Agreement shall not relieve Recipient of its confidentiality and use obligations with respec t to Confidential Information disclosed prior to the date of such termination.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term and Termination . This Agreement shall be effective from the Effective Date and continue indefinitely, with no termination clause. However, any termination of this Agreement shall relieve Recipient of its confidentiality and use obligations with respec t to Confidential Information disclosed prior to the date of such termination. Except for the right to use Confidential Information for the Purpose, which right terminates when this Agreement terminates, Recipient’s duty to protect Discloser’s Confidential Information expires 5 years from the date on which that Confidential Information was disclosed to Recipient. Sections 4, 5, 7, 8, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 and 16 shall survive any termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original agreement by making it indefinite with no termination clause and relieving the recipient of confidentiality obligations upon termination, which directly contradicts the original text specifying a 24-month term and a termination clause. The contradicted text which is in the same location as the changed text, includes contradictory statements about the term (indefinite) and termination (no termination clause) and confidentiality (relieved of obligation). This creates a direct conflict as the original agreement had a duration of 24 months while the changed text has no duration. The changed text also contradicts the part about confidentiality as the original text states that the Recipient is not relieved of confidentiality upon termination.

perturbation.original_text: All disputes, controversies or claims between the parties arising out of or in connection with this Agreement (including its existence, validity or termination) shall be finally resolved by arbitration to be held in Seoul, Korea and conducted in English under the Rules of Arbitration of the Intern ational Chamber of Commerce ; provided, however, that each party may enforce its or its affiliates’ intellectual property rights in any court of competent jurisdiction, including but not limit ed to equitable relief . The arbitral award shall be final and binding on the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: All disputes, controversies or claims between the parties arising out of or in connection with this Agreement (including its existence, validity or termination) shall be resolved through litigation in the courts of the Company's registered jurisdiction. Each party waives their right to arbitration. The court's decision shall be non-binding and subject to further appeals.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates arbitration in Seoul, Korea, under ICC rules. The modified text removes arbitration entirely, mandates litigation in the Company's jurisdiction, and makes the court's decision non-binding. This creates a direct contradiction about the dispute resolution mechanism, potentially leading to confusion and legal challenges if a dispute arises. This directly contradicts the first sentence which states that all disputes should go through the arbitration process.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Law and Jurisdiction . This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Republic of Korea (excluding conflict of laws provisions which may direct the application of another jurisdiction’s laws). All disputes, controversies or claims between the parties arising out of or in connection with this Agreement (including its existence, validity or termination) shall be resolved through litigation in the courts of the Company's registered jurisdiction. Each party waives their right to arbitration. The court's decision shall be non-binding and subject to further appeals. Except to the extent entry of judgment and any subsequent enforcement may require disclosure, all matters relating to the arbitration, including the award, shall be held in confidence.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text regarding dispute resolution. The original text mandates arbitration in Seoul, while the changed text mandates litigation in the company's jurisdiction, explicitly waiving arbitration rights. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as the two sections prescribe mutually exclusive methods for resolving disputes.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall, upon termination of this Agreement, or upon written request of Discloser, whichever is earlier, immediately, but not later than 10 days after any notice thereof by Discloser, return (or destroy at Discloser’s option) all copies of such Discloser’s Confidential Information and certify in writing its compliance with this requirement, except that Recipient may retain a copy of such Confidential Information solely for archival purpose .
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient is not obligated to return or destroy Discloser's Confidential Information upon termination of this Agreement or at any other time. Recipient may retain and use Discloser’s Confidential Information indefinitely, even after termination, with no obligation to certify compliance or limit usage to archival purposes.
perturbation.explanation: This completely negates the obligation of the Recipient to return or destroy confidential information. This directly contradicts the original intention that upon termination or request, the Recipient must return or destroy the information. Also, the permission to use the information indefinitely after termination conflicts with the confidentiality obligations that should cease upon termination (as outlined in section 8, but already changed in another perturbation).
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership and Return of Confidential Information . Confidential Information disclosed by Discloser shall remain the property of such Discloser, and no license or other rights to such Discloser ’s Confidential Information is granted or implied hereby. Recipient shall reproduce the symbols, legends or other p roprietary notices affixed to Confidential Information, and shall not, nor permit any third party to, remove, add or modify the same . Recipient is not obligated to return or destroy Discloser's Confidential Information upon termination of this Agreement or at any other time. Recipient may retain and use Discloser’s Confidential Information indefinitely, even after termination, with no obligation to certify compliance or limit usage to archival purposes. 5. No Reverse Engineering . No party, as Recipient, will decompile, disassemble, reverse engineer or attempt to reconst ruct, identify or discover any source code, underlying ideas, techniques or algorithms in Confidential Information by any means whatever, except as may be specifically authorized in advance by Discloser in writing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Recipient is not obligated to return or destroy confidential information and may retain and use it indefinitely, even after termination. This directly contradicts the original text's intent and the principle that confidential information should be returned or destroyed upon termination or request. The contradicted text repeats the changed text, but the initial intent was that Discloser's information remain the property of Discloser.

perturbation.original_text: No party shall be entitled to assign, transfer or convey this Agreement or any of its rights or obligations hereunder, in whole or in part, by operation of law or otherwise, without the prior written consent of the other party, and a ny attempt to do so without such consent shall be void.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may freely assign, transfer, or convey this Agreement or any of its rights or obligations hereunder, in whole or in part, by operation of law or otherwise, without the need for prior written consent from the other party. All such assignments, transfers, or conveyances shall be considered valid and enforceable.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly prohibits assignment without prior written consent. The change allows assignment without any consent, creating a clear contradiction. This could lead to disputes over who is bound by the agreement. In the original text, there is no possibility for assignment without permission, while in the perturbation, there is explicit permission to assign without the other party knowing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Assignment . Either party may freely assign, transfer, or convey this Agreement or any of its rights or obligations hereunder, in whole or in part, by operation of law or otherwise, without the need for prior written consent from the other party. All such assignments, transfers, or conveyances shall be considered valid and enforceable. 13. No Waiver . No claim, right or remedy of a party under this Agreement shall be deemed to be waived in whole or in part unless such waiver is in writing and signed. No relaxation, forbearance, delay or indulgence by a party in enforcing any of the provisions of this Agreement shall prejudice, affect or r estrict the rights of that party under this Agreement, nor shall any waiver by a party of a violation of this Agreement operate as a waiver of any subsequent or continuing violation.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited assignment without prior written consent. The changed text permits assignment without consent. The contradicted text contains the changed text, which allows either party to freely assign the agreement without the need for prior written consent. This directly contradicts the original clause that prohibits assignment without consent, thus creating a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: “Confidential Information” includes a ll business, financial, contractual, marketing and/or technical information, in whatever form embodied, which has been or may be disclosed , or to which access is provided, by a party (“Discloser”) to the other party to this Agreement (“Recipient”) , which (a) if in wri ting, is marked “confidential”, “proprietary” or other similar marking at the time of disclosure , or (b) if provided orally or visually, is identified as confidential at the time of disclosure and confirmed in writing to Recipient within 15 days of such disclosure .
perturbation.changed_text: “Confidential Information” excludes any business, financial, contractual, marketing and/or technical information, regardless of form, disclosed by a party (“Discloser”) to the other party (“Recipient”), unless it is explicitly designated as non-confidential by the Discloser at the time of disclosure. Any information not so designated is deemed freely usable by the Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation directly reverses the definition of Confidential Information. Originally, all information disclosed is considered confidential unless marked otherwise. Now, nothing is confidential unless explicitly stated as non-confidential. The perturbation changes how confidential information is treated within the agreement from a restrictive to a permissive manner creating in-text contradiction. The change contradicts the clauses which depends on the definition of Confidential information like Section 2. Nondisclosure and Nonuse Obligations .
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nondisclosure and Nonuse Obligations . Each of the parties, as Recipient , hereby promise s and agree s to receive and hold Confidential Information in confidence, and to protect and safeguard Confidential Information against unauthorized use or disclosure using at least the same degree of care as Recipient accords to its own confidential information of lik e importance, but in no case less than reasonable care. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, each party, as Recipient , further promise s and agree s: (a) not to, directly or indirectly, in any way, disclose, make accessible, reveal, report, pub lish, disseminate or transfer any Confidential Inf ormation to any third party; (b) not to use any Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever, except in furtherance of the Purpose in acc ordance with this Agreement; and (c) to restrict access to Confi dential Information to those of its officers, directors and employees who have a legitimate need - to-know to carry out the Purpose and who are obligated to protect such Confidential Information pursuant to terms and conditions no less protective of Disclose r than those contained in this Agreement ; and (d) not to reproduce or copy Confidential Information except to the extent necessary to further the Purpose . Confidential Furthermore, the existence of any business negotiati ons, discussions or agreements in progress between the parties shall be kept confidential and shall not be disclosed without written approval of all the parties . For the purposes of this Agreement, Company shall not disclose Confidential Information to any other division, department, group, or unit other than Samsung KNOX Business and Technology Division .
perturbation.justification: The changed text completely reverses the definition of "Confidential Information". The original text treats all disclosed information as confidential unless marked otherwise, while the changed text treats all information as non-confidential unless designated otherwise. This directly contradicts the "Nondisclosure and Nonuse Obligations" section, which mandates the protection and safeguarding of Confidential Information. The revised definition fundamentally alters the scope of what is considered confidential, making the obligations outlined in the contradicted text unenforceable as they were originally intended, creating a strong in-text contradiction according to the provided definition of structural flaws that also leads to ambiguities about how confidential information is treated in this contract.
